definitions: To commit to something with maximum effort and ambition, or not do it at all.
To pursue a goal with great enthusiasm and without reservation.
To take a bold and ambitious approach to a task or challenge.
examples: For our anniversary trip, we decided to go big or go home and booked a two-week cruise.
When starting a new business, you have to go big or go home if you want to succeed.
She told her team, 'It's time to go big or go home on this project.'
synonyms: put all your eggs in one basket
